action.reasoning: Observation: ensemble_search failed because saved checkpoints come from at least two incompatible TrafficModel variants; loading all of them into the current class causes state_dict mismatches. Analysis: the best public checkpoint ema_weighted.pt likely uses the older architecture with head/horizon_mixer keys, while current model.py is the newer end_conv variant. The highest-value low-risk move is to make predict.py architecture-aware so it can load either checkpoint family, then run a validation-time ensemble search over only compatible loading paths. This avoids retraining and can exploit existing strong checkpoints. Decision: update model.py to include both model variants and a checkpoint-driven builder, update predict.py to use it, write a robust ensemble_search.py that evaluates single models and convex blends on val.npy, locally test predict on both a .pt and an ensemble .json, then submit the best ensemble if validation improves over ema_weighted alone.
